title: Chest Pain Algorithm
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Chest Pain Algorithm
Identify key diagnostic criteria for Chest Pain Algorithm
Recognize complications and when to escalate care

sections.title: Overview & Pathophysiology
sections.content: **Chest Pain Algorithm** — This section covers the fundamental pathophysiology, epidemiology, and clinical significance. Understanding the underlying mechanisms is essential for clinical reasoning and evidence-based management.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
sections.content: Key clinical features, diagnostic criteria, and workup for Chest Pain Algorithm. This section emphasizes pattern recognition and systematic diagnostic approaches.

sections.title: Management & Treatment
sections.content: Evidence-based management strategies for Chest Pain Algorithm, including first-line therapies, escalation pathways, and monitoring parameters.

sections.title: Complications & Follow-Up
sections.content: Potential complications, prognosis, and longitudinal management considerations for Chest Pain Algorithm.

keyTakeaways: Recognize the classic presentation of Chest Pain Algorithm
Identify red flags requiring urgent intervention

reviewQuestions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected Chest Pain Algorithm?
options: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
Consult a specialist before any evaluation
reviewQuestions.explanation: A thorough history and physical examination is always the cornerstone of clinical evaluation for Chest Pain Algorithm, guiding subsequent diagnostic workup and management decisions.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the MOST critical factor in determining the management approach for Chest Pain Algorithm?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Clinical severity and appropriate risk stratification guide management decisions in Chest Pain Algorithm, determining the level of intervention and monitoring required.

reviewQuestions.question: Which complication of Chest Pain Algorithm requires the MOST urgent intervention?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Hemodynamic instability in the setting of Chest Pain Algorithm requires immediate intervention including fluid resuscitation, close monitoring, and potentially escalation to critical care.

imaging_reasoning.order_reasoning: ECG within 10 minutes of presentation; cath lab for STEMI
imaging_reasoning.expected_findings: ST elevation (STEMI), ST depression/T-wave inversion (NSTEMI), new LBBB
imaging_reasoning.management_impact: STEMI → immediate PCI (<90 min); NSTEMI → risk stratify for timing of cath
